description: Merging an adapter into base weights at the wrong scale silently corrupts the served model.
body: Failure mode: a LoRA adapter is merged into the base at a different scaling factor than it was trained with, or onto the wrong base, producing a merged checkpoint whose outputs drift from the runtime adapter-on-base behavior. Detection signals: a parity check showing material divergence between the merged model and the adapter-on-base reference, and quality regressions appearing only after the merge step. Do not deploy the merged artifact on the assumption that merge is lossless. Safer fallback: merge at the exact trained scaling against the recorded base, run a parity check on a reference set, and if parity fails, deny the merged checkpoint and serve the adapter at runtime while escalating.
